title: LCL Was Thoughts About Eva Bio
display_title: LCL Was Thoughts About Eva Bio
wikitext:
[]: LCL [Was: Thoughts About Eva Bio]             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Sun Dec 30, 2007 4:14 pm Split from here . -Reichu LCL is an amniotic fluid anyways. Wait, how is that even possible?

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Sun Dec 30, 2007 4:41 pm Anonymous_Evafan wrote: LCL is an amniotic fluid anyways. Wait, how is that even possible?

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Dec 30, 2007 5:24 pm Anonymous_Evafan wrote: LCL is an amniotic fluid anyways. Wait, how is that even possible?

[]:             Quote Post by Zuggy » Sun Dec 30, 2007 5:29 pm Yeah, LCL has some weird properties like turning into a gas when an electric current is run through it* and stuff, and it somehow helps with the pilot's synchronising with the Eva, and Rei (presumably) linking up with that big brain thingy in the dummyplug plant. *maybe this has something to do with an ATF being able to turn LCL into other elements that our bodies are composed of as well.

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Sun Dec 30, 2007 5:49 pm Reichu wrote: Does that mean her entire system runs off amniotic fluid? I mean, you can see when Eva-00 pulls the Spear out that she doesn't just bleed that stuff out of her bottom.

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Sun Dec 30, 2007 5:49 pm Is the "turning into a gas" thing official or a fanwank to explain things like the tears falling?

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Dec 30, 2007 6:05 pm D&R pamphlet wrote: When the Eva pilots are inside the Eva, they are protected by an amniotic fluid called L.C.L.

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Sun Dec 30, 2007 6:18 pm Anonymous_Evafan wrote: When the Eva pilots are inside the Eva, they are protected by an amniotic fluid called L.C.L. Before being electrically charged, L.C.L. is a yellow, viscous fluid that smells like blood. L.C.L. performs a number of functions such as linking the pilot and the Eva, supplying oxygen directly to the lungs, and protecting the pilot from both mental attacks and physical shocks.

[]:             Quote Post by Zuggy » Mon Dec 31, 2007 9:29 am NAveryW wrote: Is the "turning into a gas" thing official or a fanwank to explain things like the tears falling?

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon Dec 31, 2007 11:24 am Yep: Behavioral Logic of LCL .

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Mon Dec 31, 2007 2:19 pm It's molecular arrangement is altered in some fashion by an electrical charge. What it gets turned into... Lol sci-fi.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Mon Dec 31, 2007 4:20 pm QUANTUM MAGIC (and a little bit of Mayo)

[]:             Quote Post by THE Hal E. Burton 9000 » Mon Dec 31, 2007 4:48 pm plz, no use of THAT M WORD

[]:             Quote Post by BEsERk EVA01 » Mon Dec 31, 2007 7:23 pm Well, when they do link up the Eva with the Pilot during synchronization, they use currents or something like that to link them Pilot/Eva.

[]:             Quote Post by Zuggy » Tue Jan 01, 2008 10:34 am Anonymous_Evafan wrote: It's molecular arrangement is altered in some fashion by an electrical charge. What it gets turned into... Lol sci-fi.

[]:             Quote Post by THE Hal E. Burton 9000 » Tue Jan 01, 2008 11:02 am now where'd I put my philosopher's(/sorcerer's [Harry Potter/US reference]) stone? :P

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Tue Jan 01, 2008 11:05 am Zugzwang wrote: So, um, can electricity alter the charges of those or something and so change the element? Like in this - http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gold_synthesis#Gold_synthesis_from_Mercury - isn't the only reason making gold from mercury is possible is because it's close to it on the atomic table?

[]:             Quote Post by Zuggy » Tue Jan 01, 2008 11:07 am Hal: Aye, turned out in the end that the Stone of the Philosophers was a nuclear reactor. :P Brik: lolwut

[]:             Quote Post by THE Hal E. Burton 9000 » Tue Jan 01, 2008 11:10 am hehe, true and that show is known as Fullmetal Alchemist, go see it, it's kind of good

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Tue Jan 01, 2008 11:12 am Zugzwang wrote: Brik: lolwut
